weather_line: Clear above the ridgeline · something stirs in the garage

stories.headline: A Waymarker Circle Appears on the Table: Steve Produces a Map from the Garage at Quarter Past Seven; Pencil Is Involved; the Jeep Has Been Heard Humming to Itself
body: The kitchen table, cleared of all prior obligations since Thursday afternoon, received its first new occupant at seven seventeen this morning: a folded ordnance chart, weathered at the creases, which Steve retrieved from somewhere in the garage that he has declined to name. A single waymarker — coordinates unconfirmed, ridge designation withheld pending further reconnaissance — has been circled in pencil. The pencil is a 2H. This detail is considered significant by those who know.

The Jeep, for its part, has been heard producing a low, self-satisfied hum in the vicinity of the drive since approximately six o'clock. Mechanical analysts note that this particular frequency has historically preceded a departure within seventy-two hours. Steve has not confirmed this. He has, however, refilled the water flasks, which is its own kind of confirmation.
The table has made no public statement. Sources close to the table describe it as, quote, 'ready.'

stories.headline: Friday Reaches the Bank; Christine Has Already Seen It Coming; Two Outstanding Matters Settled Before It Finishes Arriving
body: Friday morning presented itself at Gringotts with the usual mixture of unresolved carryover and optimistic new correspondence, and was received by the Liaison Wing with its customary brisk efficiency. Two matters flagged in Thursday's close-of-business summary were resolved before the corridor clock had completed its opening sequence — the clock, which runs eighteen seconds long and has done so since 1993, did not finish in time.
The Duty Goblin updated the log. He used Christine's preferred ink. He has not explained why.

stories.headline: Garage Reconnaissance Conducted Without Authorisation; One Dog Unaccounted For Between Seven and Seven-Twelve; Map Corners Remain Suspiciously Damp
body: An unverified source — described only as 'small, brown, and extremely purposeful' — is believed to have conducted an unsanctioned survey of the garage between seven o'clock and seven twelve this morning, immediately before Steve retrieved the ordnance chart. Whether the chart's lower left corner arrived damp as a result of this survey, or whether the dampness predates it, remains an open question that no one in the household has formally raised.
Lucy has been seen studying the pencilled waymarker circle from a distance of approximately four feet. She has not touched it. This, experts agree, is the most suspicious thing about the situation.

stories.headline: Ministry Issues Friday Bulletin Confirming Friday; Notes That the Week Has Concluded 'In a Manner Broadly Consistent With It Having Occurred'; Attaches No Photographs for the First Time in Eleven Days
body: The Ministry of Magic released its end-of-week summary at five past nine, confirming that Friday is occurring and that the preceding four days did, in fact, constitute a week. The bulletin described the period as 'broadly productive' without specifying for whom. Observers noted the absence of any attached photograph — the first such omission since Monday the thirteenth — and have offered no consensus on what this means.
A spokesperson confirmed that the Ministry is 'aware of the map' and is 'monitoring the situation with appropriate interest.' The spokesperson did not say how they know about the map. No one asked.

familiars_corner: Lucy: Was observed near the garage at seven o'clock, in the garage at seven-four, and back at her bed by seven-twelve, apparently unbothered. The map's damp corner has been attributed to 'condensation' by the only witness, who is Lucy.
Ellie: Noticed the map on the table, approached it with great interest, and attempted to greet it. The map did not respond. Ellie has concluded the map is shy and is giving it space to warm up.

Jersey: Has identified the map as an excellent surface for resting one's chin while gazing soulfully at whoever is in the kitchen. Has deployed this technique twice already. Considers the morning a success.
founding_editor_note: If the map's got a crease that won't lie flat, run a warm iron over it — cotton setting, no steam. I built the press table out of a solid door blank and it never warped once. The pencil circle looks straight. Good start. — D.

in_brief.sport: Friday Intramural Weightlifting: all records stand; the records have been told they may stand until Monday, at which point the matter will be revisited.
in_brief.forecast: Clear through the weekend, with a strong probability of tyres on gravel by Sunday morning and something good at the end of a ridge.

storyline_updates.title: The Table Awaits
storyline_updates.summary: Day 2: The table now hosts an ordnance chart, retrieved from the garage at seven seventeen by Steve, with a single waymarker circled in 2H pencil. The Jeep has been humming since six. The water flasks are refilled. Lucy conducted unsanctioned garage reconnaissance beforehand; the map's lower left corner arrived damp; no one has raised this officially. The Ministry is aware of the map. Day 3 should bring either the destination named (a ridge, a gorge, a named trail in misty Appalachian country), or additional items joining the map on the table — a trail guide, a spare part, something from the garage that clarifies the nature of the expedition. The departure window is narrowing toward the weekend.
